Hey guys, JB here. It's time for your daily motivation slash skills training. We're going for skills today. Topic is a big one. It's looping. When you ask for the order for the first time, just remember, there's only three things that a prospect can say. They can say yes as in, yeah, let's go, let's do it, which in case you have a lay down sale, right, one of the perfect lay downs where basically they were pre-sold before they entered the encounter. So they entered at a very high level of certainty. You said a couple of things that said, yep, this is what I want. This is what I need. And boom, they bought. Those people have a very low action threshold and they entered at a high level of certainty. So your initial presentation was enough. No objections. Bam. Kind of rare though. And depending on what you're selling, you know, in some cases, yeah, if you're selling iPhones or some sort of, you know, commodity based, I would say so well known and you're almost more of an order taker. Yeah, you can have those pretty frequently. But for people who want to influence based sales, we have to close. They're very far from you between most people. You have some objection, right? So the second group, you have yes, yeah, lay down. Number two, they say no, not interested. That's even rarer than your lay downs. Why is that? Because you already filtered these people out during the intelligence gathering phase. And this is important. In other words, you don't want to be making full blown presentations to people who don't need your product, want your product and better be a product, but afford your product, right? So how'd you find out during intelligence gathering? You took the buyers in power, buyers in heat, you move them forward during your transition, made presentations to those people and you left the look you lose and the mistakes behind. Basic stuff, right? Now think about the logic here. So now you have these buyers in heat, buyers in power, moving down the straight line. They've already answered all your intelligence gathering questions in a way that said they need the product, they want the product, they can afford it. That's why they're buyers in heat or powers or else they wouldn't be, right? That's how they got categorized. And now you make them this beautiful airtight logical case and you ask for the order for the first, what does it say? No, I change my, they don't say no. They'll say, oh wow, it sounds pretty good. Let me think about it. And that takes you to the third thing they could say, which is maybe. In other words, maybe is a catch all for all of the common objections. Maybe I'll buy, maybe I won't. That's what let me think about it means maybe I'll buy, maybe I won't buy. Bad time yet, maybe I'll buy. In other words, that's what those objections represent. Maybe. Yes, a lay down, very rare. No almost never happens at this point. You get your nose while you're cold calling, while you're doing your marketing, when you're sifting through leads, you're not interested. They never belong to the pipeline in the first place. The ones that you present to should have already been filtered out enough that they don't say no. They're not interested. They say, sounds good. Let me call you back. Sounds good. Let me speak to someone else. You get it? So you don't get nose. Sometimes you get yeses, very rare most industries, what you get tons of, of only is maybe. Maybe I'll buy, maybe I won't, which is the catch all for all your common objections. And the reason it's important for you to understand this is because your attitude in here, what you're feeling and so you're like, oh, another, oh no. He wants to think about, oh, Penny, Penny, the sky's falling. I went through this whole presentation and I got another object. I'm exaggerating a bit, but the point is you have to be ready for that first objection, expecting it and saying to yourself, now is the time. I'm going to roll up my sleeves and earn my paycheck. That's what a real salesman does. And that's where his mindset is. So you're not put off by that first objection. You eat that first objection for breakfast because now you know you can roll up your sleeves and really do your job, which is to take people who are sitting on the fence and turn them into yeses. That's what a real salesperson does. We don't take the nose and turn them into yeses. We take the, let me think about it. Let me call your backs. Bad time of the year. Let me talk to my wife. She's turned those people into yeses. And we are ready and we feel strong when we get hit with that first objection because we're expecting it. That's the distinction. I love you all. It's a big tip for the weekend going in. Remember you get stronger with the first objection because that's where the sale truly begins. Yes, from the first word out of my mouth, yes, I'm closing, but our real influence begins when they hit you with the first objection.
